items.title: Central African Republic: Elections in one of the world's poorest nations | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Voters in the Central African Republic go to the polls on Sunday in nationwide elections. They'll be casting ballots for president and for lawmakers at all levels. The country is still recovering from civil wars over a decade ago. Today the Central African Republic remains one of the world's poorest nations.

items.title: Russian drone attacks ‘designed to break the will of the people’ – former Ukraine adviser
items.author: Ayshah Tull
items.content: We spoke to Yuriy Sak – former advisor to the Ukrainian Defence Minister.

items.title: Zelenskyy to propose 60-day ceasefire in Trump meeting
items.author: Harry Fawcett
items.content:
Volodymyr Zelenskyy is already on his way to Canada for talks with western allies ahead of tomorrow’s meeting with Mr Trump.

He told reporters Ukraine was “doing everything it can to stop this war”.

Not so Russia, which launched an onslaught on Kyiv, targeting the city with missiles and drones through the night and into this morning.

Russia’s defence ministry says it’s downed over 100 drones in a Ukrainian counter attack.

items.title: Thailand and Cambodia agree to new ceasefire deal
items.author: Jane Deith
items.content:
Thailand and Cambodia have signed a ceasefire to end weeks of fierce fighting along their border – which forced a million people to flee their homes.

The 72 hour truce was agreed by the two countries after an intervention by China.

Donald Trump had claimed much of the credit for a previous ceasefire deal which collapsed last month.

items.title: Myanmar's junta holds first general election in 5 years: Why are critics questioning its legitimacy?
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>People in Myanmar are preparing to head to the polls this weekend, amid a civil war and one of Asia's worst humanitarian crises. Sunday's poll is the first of three phases of the election, which has been heavily criticized by the United Nations and Southeast Asia's ASEAN bloc. Opponents say the election is an attempt by the ruling military junta to win legitimacy on the global stage. The vote is further complicated by the junta's lack of control over all of Myanmar's territory. It's fighting a civil war that according to UN estimates has killed more than 6,000 civilians and displaced 3.6 million people.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Political situation in Myanmar
2:13 Mark Farmaner, Director of Burma Campaign UK, on the junta's goal in holding these elections

#myanmarelection #myanmarjunta
#humanitariancrisismyanmar

items.title: Why does loneliness and isolation particularly affect the younger generations? | DW Reporter
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Young people are more connected than ever – but have never felt so isolated. Why does loneliness particularly affect the generation under 30?

Studies show that almost half of young adults feel lonely. A few years ago, the figures were significantly lower. We spend time with Lalena, a DJ with thousands of social media followers, who parties with the crowds at night and fears the silence of the morning after. We meet Sean, who lost his way in the world of video games before realizing he had to make a fresh start. And we talk to Jana and Noah, looking for support after personal loss. Experts warn that social media can’t create or replace real human interaction. And they encourage young people to recognize that loneliness isn’t a sign of weakness. It is human. What helps? Openness, genuine encounters – and sometimes music or sport.

Chapter 
00:00 Why do young people feel lonely?
00:25 Lalena: DJ life between glamor and silence
02:05 Social media & loneliness: the deception
03:35 Creativity as therapy: music to relieve isolation
05:15 From gaming to a new sense of community
07:45 Ask the experts: does media consumption make us lonely?
09:05 Grief and the search for support
10:45 Young Supporters: a group effort
11:35 What really helps: sport, openness, genuine encounters
12:15 Loneliness is human — not a weakness


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: What are the terms of the new Thailand-Cambodia ceasefire agreement? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Thailand and Cambodia have agreed to a ceasefire to end weeks of deadly border clashes. In a joint statement, both sides have promised to freeze troop movements and allow civilians living in border areas to return home.
A long-standing border conflict between the neighboring countries reignited this month. The fighting is believed to have killed more than 100 and displaced hundreds of thousands of people. The dispute stems largely from a century-old map, created when Cambodia was still under French colonial rule.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Thailand and Cambodia agree to truce
0:38 Tommy Walker, journalist reporting from Chanthaburi, Thailand, on whether the deal is likely to hold

items.title: China's 'nuclear move' after Trump's "Liberation Day" | Berlin Briefing Podcast
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>2025 has exposed Germany's dependence on China, as a source of rare earths and as a market and production site for its carmakers and other major industries. Berlin says it's committed to "de-risking" the relationship. But is it serious? Or is it in too deep? As Chancellor Friedrich Merz prepares to visit Xi Jinping in early 2026, Richard Walker sat down with experts Noah Barkin and Clifford Coonan to analyze Germany's options.

items.title: Russia bombards Ukraine with missiles and drones ahead of Trump-Zelenskyy meeting Sunday | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>At least 11 people have been injured in the Ukrainian capital Kyiv after Russia launched a major missile and drone attack on the city. An air raid alert was in force in Kyiv overnight, as the mayor warned of explosions and told people to stay in shelters. Ukraine's Air Force confirmed Russian drones were targeting Kyiv along with regions in the northeast and south. The strikes come a day before Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is set to meet with US President Trump in Florida. 

DW Special Correspondent Nimisha Jaiswal reports from Kyiv.

#trumpzelenskyy
#ukraine #russiandrones

items.title: Inside a new plan to bring tech talent into the federal government
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>If you're a high-skilled tech worker, then potentially huge fortunes await you working for a startup or one of the booming AI giants. But the government needs these types of workers too.
On this episode of the Odd Lots podcast, Scott Kupor, director of the US Office of Personnel Management, joins Tracy Alloway and Joe Weisenthal to unpack his new plan to attract top talent for a two-year stint of solving problems across the bureaus http://apple.co/4lTgFT6</article>

items.title: Bringing High-Speed Internet to South Africa's Townships
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Access to fast, affordable internet is still out of reach for millions of South Africans, especially in townships. For nearly a quarter of the country's residents, digital infrastructure has changed little since the end of Apartheid. Startup Fibertime is seeking to bring high-speed connectivity to communities that have been left behind.

items.title: Nigeria confirms US help in fight against terrorism | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Nigeria has denied that the US military's Christmas Day airstrikes were targeting a specific religion. The US launched several attacks against Islamic State militants in northwestern Nigeria. President Trump said the strikes aimed to protect Christians who he claimed were being 'slaughtered' by the militants. However, the Nigerian government, which cooperated with the US action, claims that Islamist insurgents target both Christians and Muslims.

Chapters:
0:00 Nigeria denies religion determined US strike targets
2:50 Miriam Adah, Assistant Research Manager Africa at the NGO ACLED

items.title: Trump orders attacks on ISIS targets in Nigeria #politics #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>The US launched a military strike in Nigeria against Islamic State targets in a security and intelligence collaboration with the African nation's government.
President Donald Trump said he directed American forces to carry out "a powerful and deadly strike" against ISIS, and Nigeria's foreign affairs ministry confirmed the "precision hits on terrorist targets."
Laura Davison reports.</article>

items.title: Baby Bonds vs. Trump Accounts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>A new savings vehicle, dubbed “Trump accounts,” is designed to help the rising generation of American children build wealth into adulthood. Under the multitrillion-dollar tax and spending bill signed by President Donald Trump in July, the federal government will contribute $1,000 to accounts set up for every American baby born in the next few years. Darrick Hamilton, Henry Cohen Professor of Economics and Urban Policy at the New School, is behind a comparable concept known as "baby bonds," which he sees as a means to granting more social mobility and agency to young people regardless of family income levels. Professor Hamilton compares and contrasts his idea with the Trump administration's mechanism with Alexis Christoforous and Isabelle Lee on Bloomberg Businessweek Daily.
--------
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

items.title: Ellison, Paramount Put Pressure on WBD for a Deal
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Warner Bros. Discovery shares slid early Friday in premarket trading after a report from the New York Post said that Paramount Skydance could walk away from its $30-per-share cash bid and instead litigate against the company’s board for how it handled the process. But with Larry Ellison personally backing the Paramount bid, the pressure is still on. Bloomberg's Chris Palmeri reports.
--------

items.title: Gold, Silver Smash Records as End-Of-Year Rally Continues
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Gold, silver, and platinum extended a historic end-of-year rally for precious metals, jumping to all-time highs, with spot gold peaking above $4,530 an ounce. Tatiana Darie has more on Bloomberg Television.

items.title: Snow Storm Brings Holiday Travel Disruptions to NYC Airports
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>New York’s major airports have seen hundreds of flight cancellations as a significant winter storm descends on the city and neighboring regions. New York City is in line to get 5 to 9 inches (13 to 23 cm) of snow Friday into Saturday, according to the National Weather Service. Bill Wadell of AccuWeather has the details on Bloomberg Television.
--------

items.title: Tesla Faces New Door Handle Probe
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Tesla is facing a new US government probe over the door handles of its Model 3 sedans, people familiar with the matter tell Bloomberg. Steve Westly, a former Tesla board member and managing partner of The Westly Group, speaks with Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Tech."</article>

items.title: US Aggression Near Venezuela is 'Oil Based,' Says Denver Riggleman
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Former Representative Denver Riggleman said that recent moves by the US military to sure up capabilities around Latin America are driven by oil and not drug trafficking, as the Trump administration ramps up aggression in the region. Riggleman also gives his perspective on the divisions within the Republican party as it contends differing views on the Epstein files and international conflict.
--------

items.title: Holiday Spending Was Practical, Predictable Says Julia Wilson
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Julia Wilson, principal of consumer and retail strategy at KPMG, said that practical items like sweaters and gift cards were at the top of consumers' shopping lists this holiday season. Wilson also said that for tricky post-holiday tasks like returning or exchanging gifts, consumers are turning to chatbots for help.

items.title: Reasonable to be Optimistic About Markets in 2026, Says Rick Gardner
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Rick Gardner, CIO of RGA Investments, breaks down his outlook heading into the new year. Garnder says that its reasonable for investors to be 'optimistic' about markets in 2026, expecting uncertainty around policy to die down. Gardner said that as tech trade narrows, there is opportunity for investors to diversify outside of the sector.

items.title: California braces for more extreme weather | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The US state of California is bracing for even more extreme weather after wind, rain and snow pummeled the state. At least two people were killed in storm-related incidents. Officials are warning the danger is not over with flash flooding and avalanches possible.

00:00 California hit by powerful storm 
02:11 Mathew Cappucci, Meteorologist

items.title: The Year, According to Tracy Alloway, Felix Salmon, and Robert Smith
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Elon Musk has been advocating for people to have more babies. Many countries like Japan and Germany offer financial rewards to encourage childbirths. Should the rest of the world be doing the same?
On this episode of the Everybody's Business podcast, Felix Salmon (Slate Money), Robert Smith (Business History, Planet Money) and Tracy Alloway (Odd Lots), join @svaneksmith and @mchafkin to discuss the declining global fertility rate https://t.co/SkK727QEvR</article>

items.title: Zelenskiy to Meet Trump on Sunday in Florida to Discuss Peace Plan
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>John Herbst, former U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine and senior director at the Atlantic Council’s Eurasia Center, says negotiations between the U.S. and Ukraine have made progress, but notes that Vladimir Putin has rejected every proposal so far. Speaking with Joe Mathieu on "Balance of Power," Herbst adds that China is closely watching the war and does not want Putin to lose.
--------

items.title: Zelenskyy to travel to Florida for Trump meeting | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he'll meet with US President Donald Trump in Florida on Sunday, as diplomatic efforts to end the Russia-Ukraine war intensify. The US media outlet Axios reports that Zelensky is ready to hold a referendum on a potential peace plan. That would allow Ukrainains to approve concessions now prohibited by law, like giving up territory.

00:00 Zelenskyy to travel to Florida for Trump meeting
04:07 Anna Marveeva, Russia Institute, King's College London

items.title: Taiwan holds drill to boost response after metro stabbing | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Authorities in Taiwan aren't taking chances following a deadly stabbing earlier this month. As part of a security drill, police and firefighters tested their level of preparedness during a simulated mass attack inside Taipei's metro system.
